methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $510K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
GenFab Co is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Matt Parlmer that has raised $510K to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, alongside 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ).

Of the two valuation methods considered, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the company's $510K total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $1.275M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Robotics & Manufacturing universe—while 11 same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering criteria of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier needed to support a reliable median. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias.

The single-method triangulation supports a range of $1.08M to $1.47M, with a median of $1.28M. This analysis carries material uncertainty: the absence of tight peer comparables and reliance on a single methodological input limit conviction. Key unknowns include GenFab's revenue trajectory, burn rate, and competitive positioning relative to the private peer set. A more robust valuation would benefit from additional Seed-stage robotics comparables or direct traction metrics.
